name: Dart (Task Manager) MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Dart task management MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Dart — an AI-native project management tool — creating and managing tasks, tracking projects, updating task status, and integrating Dart's AI-powered project management into agent-driven workflow automation and productivity systems.
use_cases: Creating and assigning tasks from workflow automation agents
Tracking project progress and status from project management agents
Updating task completion and priority from productivity agents
Managing team workloads from coordination agents
Integrating AI coding agents with project task tracking
Building automated task creation from code review and analysis agents
not_for: Teams using Jira, Linear, or Asana (use dedicated MCPs for those)
Simple personal task lists (Dart is team/project focused)
best_when: A team using Dart's AI-native project management needs AI agents to create and manage tasks — official integration for seamless Dart workflow automation.
avoid_when: Your team uses different project management tools — use their respective MCPs.

auth.notes: Dart API key required. Generate from Dart account settings. Official MCP from Dart team.

pricing.notes: Dart has a free tier. MCP server is free open source. Official from Dart team.

performance.rate_limits: Dart API rate limits — see documentation

known_agent_gotchas: Dart account and API key required — sign up at itsdart.com
Task/project IDs required for updates — agents must track Dart entity IDs
Official MCP from Dart team — good quality but newer project
Dart's data model (tasks, sprints, assignees) must be understood
Webhook support available for reactive workflows — not MCP-based
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key required — store securely. Official Dart MCP. Team task data — apply appropriate access control.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key auth. Official Dart MCP. Team data — appropriate access control.
